channel.title: OT/ICS Threat Intel - Manufacturing
channel.description: Curated OT/ICS advisories, incidents and threat intelligence. Editorial by Mike Holcomb in partnership with UtilSec.

item.title: [Critical] Manufacturing is the ransomware bullseye — about 62% of industrial victims in Q1 2026, and an OT halt can hit within hours
item.description: Manufacturing has become the ransomware bullseye. Industrial-ransomware tracking put manufacturing at roughly 62% of all industrial victims in the first quarter of 2026 — hundreds of incidents in a single quarter — and Dragos' 2026 report likewise found manufacturing absorbing about two-thirds of OT-relevant ransomware, with dwell times measured in weeks. What makes the sector so exposed is that a crew only has to reach the seam between IT and the plant: once ransomware crosses into OT, production can halt within hours, and a stopped line bleeds revenue immediately. The groups are numerous and rotating, and increasingly reach in through stolen credentials and engineering workstations rather than exotic exploits. The defensible posture is boringly effective — segment IT from OT, lock down remote access, and keep tested, isolated backups you can actually restore. - Cybersecurity Media / Dragos - Q1 2026 industrial-ransomware tracking; Dragos 2026 report

item.title: [High] Doppel: manufacturing attacks are shifting toward identity — leaked credentials and vishing lead the way in
item.description: Security firm Doppel warned in mid-June that cyber threats against manufacturers are increasingly identity-driven, with leaked credentials and voice-phishing (vishing) campaigns emerging as primary entry vectors. The pattern matters because it matches how ransomware crews actually operate: rather than burning exploits, they authenticate with valid credentials and then work toward the plant floor, where an engineering workstation or remote-access account can bridge into OT. For manufacturers the implication is that identity hygiene — MFA everywhere, aggressive credential rotation, help-desk verification against vishing, and monitoring for anomalous logins — is now front-line OT defense, not just IT housekeeping. - Doppel / Industrial Cyber - Reported 19 Jun 2026

item.title: [High] Dragos 2026: manufacturing takes about two-thirds of OT ransomware, and new groups target engineering workstations
item.description: Dragos' 2026 report frames manufacturing as the most-targeted OT sector, absorbing roughly two-thirds of ransomware impacting industrial organizations, against a backdrop of a growing number of ransomware groups and dwell times measured in weeks. It also details newly tracked adversaries — including AZURITE, focused on OT engineering workstations to exfiltrate operational data and maintain long-term access — alongside a broader shift from data theft toward operational disruption. For plant defenders the takeaways are concrete: treat the engineering workstation as a crown-jewel asset, watch the IT/OT boundary for lateral movement, and assume adversaries may already be resident when planning detection and recovery. - Dragos - Dragos 2026 OT/ICS report

item.title: [High] CISA updates its Mitsubishi Electric CNC Series advisory — check the machine tools on your floor
item.description: CISA's 2 July batch included an updated advisory (Update D) for the Mitsubishi Electric CNC Series, the computer-numerical-control platform that runs machining centers across discrete manufacturing. CNCs sit directly on production and are frequently networked for program transfer and monitoring, so a reachable vulnerability can mean downtime or tampering with machining programs. Review the advisory for affected models and firmware, apply Mitsubishi's fixes or mitigations, and keep CNC networks segmented from business systems. - CISA ICS Advisories - Advisory 2 Jul 2026 (Update D)

item.title: [High] Mitsubishi Electric MELSOFT Update Manager flagged — a foothold in the engineering toolchain
item.description: CISA's 30 June advisories covered the Mitsubishi Electric MELSOFT Update Manager (SW1DND-UDM-M), the utility that keeps engineering software current. Toolchain and update components are high-value because they run on engineering workstations with broad access to controllers, so a flaw here can undercut the very systems used to program the plant. Confirm whether the Update Manager is deployed, apply the vendor's fixes, and treat engineering-software hosts as sensitive. - CISA ICS Advisories - Advisory 30 Jun 2026

item.title: [Medium] Delta Electronics DVP12SE PLC advisory — common in packaging and process skids
item.description: CISA's 30 June batch also covered the Delta Electronics DVP12SE programmable logic controller, a compact PLC found across packaging lines and process skids in discrete and process manufacturing. Confirm whether any DVP12SE units are in your lines, apply the vendor's fixes, and ensure the controllers are segmented away from business and internet-facing networks. - CISA ICS Advisories - Advisory 30 Jun 2026

item.title: [High] FortiBleed: ~86,644 Fortinet firewalls and VPNs under active attack — the remote-access path into engineering networks
item.description: CISA is urging Fortinet operators to act after roughly 86,644 internet-facing FortiGate firewalls and SSL-VPN gateways had their credentials exposed in the FortiBleed leak and put to use in active attacks. For manufacturers the danger is the remote-access layer: these appliances often front vendor and engineer access into plant and engineering networks, and because credentials leaked rather than a bug being exploited, there is no patch to apply. Valid stolen credentials are exactly how ransomware crews get in, so treat any exposed device as compromised and rotate now. - CISA (hardening alert) - CISA alert 18 Jun 2026 — active exploitation

item.title: [Policy] NIST SP 1339 OT Backup Quick Start Guide targets faster line recovery
item.description: NIST released an OT Backup Quick Start Guide (SP 1339) on 19 June to help industrial operators build recoverable backups and speed incident recovery. For manufacturers weighing how quickly ransomware can halt a line, tested and isolated backups of PLC, HMI and engineering-workstation configurations are the difference between a restore measured in hours and a rebuild measured in weeks. Use it to benchmark your OT backup coverage and recovery timelines. - NIST / Industrial Cyber - Guide released 19 Jun 2026
